{"society_power": 3.5, "state_power": 2.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Absent Leviathan losing ground to guerrillas, paras, drug economy", "reasoning": "The 1991 Constitution and M-19 demobilization strengthened legitimate civic participation, but this gain was offset by drug-fueled fragmentation of society, so society holds roughly steady around moderate-weak. State power slid further as guerrilla territorial expansion, paramilitary consolidation (AUC), military defeats, and the Proceso 8000 corruption scandal hollowed out its monopoly of violence and institutional legitimacy."}
